records.ety: [A word brought by the English from India; of uncertain origin; cf. Skr. varaa, Pg. varanda, Sp. baranda, Malay baranda.]

defs: An open, roofed gallery or portico, adjoining a dwelling house, forming an out-of-door sitting room. See Loggia.
quotes: The house was of adobe, low, with a wide veranda on the three sides of the inner court.
